sourceMovie.title: Rape Zombie: Lust of the Dead
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Rape Zombie: Lust of the Dead (2012) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Naoyuki Tomomatsu's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Warm Bodies
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Rape Zombie: Lust of the Dead clicked for you, Warm Bodies (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Rape Zombie: Lust of the Dead?
faq.answer: Try Warm Bodies, Braindead, Fright Night—each shares DNA with Rape Zombie: Lust of the Dead while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Rape Zombie: Lust of the Dead?
faq.answer: Rape Zombie: Lust of the Dead (2012) is best described as Comedy, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Rape Zombie: Lust of the Dead part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Rape Zombie Series" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
